---
title: "Where AI fails — a field guide"
---

# Where AI fails — a field guide

::: {.callout-warning}
## Placeholder chapter
Stub. To be drafted via the chapter-writing skill.
:::

## What this chapter will cover

The preface gave the headlines on where AI bluffs. This chapter is the field guide: a taxonomy of the specific failure modes you will meet, with a one-line diagnostic for each.

- **Hallucinated facts** — citations to papers that don't exist, statistics that look right and aren't, function names from libraries that don't have them.
- **Confidently wrong code** — code that runs and gives a plausible answer to a different question than the one you asked.
- **Silent data loss** — joins that drop rows, filters that keep too many, sample restrictions applied to one half of a script and not the other.
- **Causal-sounding correlation** — a regression coefficient described as an "effect" when nothing in the design supports a causal reading.
- **Stale knowledge** — APIs, library versions, regulations, sports results. Anything that moved after the model's training cut-off.
- **Audience drift** — the report drifts toward a generic data-science blog tone instead of the specific reader you have.

For each, one diagnostic the reader can apply in under a minute.

## Where this chapter fits

Closes Part I. After this, the reader should be able to read AI output sceptically without reading it cynically.

## TODO for the author

- One real example per failure mode. Real outputs, names anonymised where needed.
- A "diagnostic ladder": cheapest check first, more expensive checks only if the cheap ones flag a problem.
- A note that the failure modes evolve with the models. Date everything.
